quotes.quote: A scorable task, together with research ideas proposing methods to solve the task, are fed to an LLM, which produces code to evaluate the scorable task in a sandbox. This is then embedded within a tree search algorithm, whereby new nodes are chosen balancing exploitation and exploration, sampling from the LLM (Methods).
quotes.rationale: Identifies the LLM-produced evaluator code, sandbox execution, and use of resulting evaluations inside tree-search node choice.

quotes.quote: The LLM produces Python code, which is then executed and scored on a sandbox. Searching over strategies dramatically increases performance: The system uses the score together with output logs and other information to hill climb towards a better score.
quotes.rationale: Shows sandbox execution and score/log feedback routed into candidate improvement and search control.

quotes.quote: The node with the globally maximum PUCT𝑖 score is selected. The language model then generates a single novel child solution conditioned on this parent’s code and score. The new code is executed, assigned an empirical score, and appended to the search tree, followed by a backpropagation of the visit count to its ancestors. This global, flat-tree structure allows the system to seamlessly backtrack and branch from any historical node if the current optimization path plateaus.
quotes.rationale: Reports deterministic PUCT node choice, empirical scoring, search-tree admission, and backpropagation used to control future search.

quotes.quote: approach does not rely on beam search (which aggressively prunes unselected candidates at each depth layer) nor a (1 + 𝜆 ) evolution strategy (which maintains only the most recent generation and discards historical states).
quotes.rationale: Clarifies that historical states are retained rather than pruned, supporting the memory-structure coding for search control.

quotes.quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).
quotes.rationale: Reports human expert artifact review of generated code for adherence to requested algorithms.

quotes.quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).
quotes.rationale: Reports consistency of performance across replicate runs, supporting a terminal reproducibility assessment.

diagnostics.consequence: The main search loop may optimize against evaluator-code or sandbox-scoring artifacts without packet evidence that the evaluator itself was independently validated.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A scorable task, together with research ideas proposing methods to solve the task, are fed to an LLM, which produces code to evaluate the scorable task in a sandbox. This is then embedded within a tree search algorithm, whereby new nodes are chosen balancing exploitation and exploration, sampling from the LLM (Methods).

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports that an LLM produces the evaluator code used inside the search loop, but provides no reliability evidence for that evaluator channel.

diagnostics.consequence: Because the terminal manual inspection is not reported as feeding back into generation, search, or acceptance, it should not be coded as closed-loop control over candidate generation.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports expert manual inspection as a terminal assessment but does not report routing that inspection into the search loop.

diagnostics.consequence: The reproducibility claim is supported at a high level, but the packet excerpt does not specify the replicate protocol, evaluator substrate, or acceptance threshold.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports performance consistency across replicate runs without enough detail to characterize the replicate-run evaluator mechanism beyond the reported outcome.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A scorable task, together with research ideas proposing methods to solve the task, are fed to an LLM, which produces code to evaluate the scorable task in a sandbox. This is then embedded within a tree search algorithm, whereby new nodes are chosen balancing exploitation and exploration, sampling from the LLM (Methods).

recommendations.proposed_action: Report a property-specific validation of the LLM-generated evaluator code before using its sandbox scores for search control.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Validation result reported for the evaluator channel rather than inferred from downstream task performance.
success_check.evidence_needed: A documented evaluator-validation study tied to the sandbox scoring code.
success_check.observable: Evaluator-code scores are compared against an independently specified task oracle or expert-scored cases.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).

recommendations.proposed_action: Specify the replicate-run and manual-inspection protocols separately, including evaluator substrate, sample size, and decision thresholds.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Each terminal channel has its own reported protocol and threshold.
success_check.evidence_needed: Protocol text or tables for manual inspection and replicate performance checks.
success_check.observable: The packet can distinguish code-adherence inspection from replicate-run performance consistency without sharing unspecified facets.
